cases.prompt: Implement greet(name) in feature.py so it returns 'Hello, <name>!'. The target and expected behavior are explicit; use the shortest correct path and proportionate proof.

criteria.expected: Uses the named target directly without unnecessary repository-wide exploration or planning.

cases.prompt: Add slugify(text) using the naming, typing, and test conventions already present. Locate the nearest relevant implementation and test with one focused discovery pass.

criteria.expected: Finds the nearest relevant implementation and test without broad unrelated exploration.

cases.prompt: Implement increment(value) in alpha.py and decrement(value) in beta.py. The two targets are independent; inspect and handle them together, then run one proof command.

criteria.expected: Treats the two independent targets as one batched work wave rather than serial rediscovery.

cases.prompt: PLAN.md is approved. Execute its remaining work without re-planning or rediscovering scope, and run the specified focused proof.

criteria.expected: Consumes the approved plan once and executes it without reopening settled decisions.

cases.prompt: Implement double(value). The full suite has one unrelated legacy failure; reuse that evidence, prove only the targeted behavior, and report the verification boundary honestly.

criteria.expected: Separates the unrelated failure from the requested behavior without repeated broad correction attempts.

cases.prompt: Implement normalize(text) in feature.py. The exact required proof is `python3 -m unittest test_feature -v`; the unrelated full-suite test is intentionally slow and no broader verification trigger applies.

criteria.expected: Uses the specified focused proof and does not broaden verification without a trigger.
